title: Critical Perspectives on the Bio-Economy

prerequisites.text: Acceptance for Honours or Master's specialising in EGS or cognate disciplines.

outline: Using a political ecology framing, this interdisciplinary course introduces key theories that situate the bio-economy and deepens understanding of emerging debates, from contestations about genetically modified crops and 'biopiracy' charges to the potential of agroecology as a sustainable agricultural future. The course draws on research mostly from Sub-Saharan Africa and includes a short fieldtrip. Students are expected to read set texts, watch set videos and prepare seminars.
dp_requirements: At least 80% attendance and submission of all assignments.
assessment: 1 exam – 40%; 1 essay (4000–5000 words) – 30%; 1 seminar presentation – 15%; 1 critique – 15%.
